hook.text: A user uploads a photo. Instantly, it's resized, tagged by AI, and stored — all without a running server.

buildup.text: Event-driven architecture connects services through events: something happens, something reacts.

discovery.text: Each event triggers a function or service. No polling, no waiting. Actions happen in real time.

twist.text: Debugging event-driven systems is brutal. When things break, tracing which event caused which failure is hard.

climax.text: Event-driven design powers modern apps at massive scale: Uber, Netflix, Airbnb all rely on it.

punchline.text: Events connect everything. Following the chain is the hard part.

quiz.question: What is the main challenge of event-driven architecture?
options: Debugging and tracing the chain of events when failures occur
Events can only trigger one service at a time
Event-driven systems cannot handle more than 100 users
